blocking_defect_fixed_during_pass.evidence: information_schema.role_table_grants returned 0 rows for facilitation_cases / facilitation_case_events / facilitation_case_evidence
blocking_defect_fixed_during_pass.fix: Applied migration granting SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE to authenticated and ALL to service_role on the three facilitation tables. Without this the storage RLS policy fevd_select/fevd_insert (which subqueries facilitation_cases) could not be evaluated by the authenticated role.

remaining_failure.body: {"statusCode":"503","error":"DatabaseInvalidObjectDefinition","message":"The database schema is invalid or incompatible."}
remaining_failure.likely_cause: Storage service's policy planner is failing to compile the EXISTS subquery against public.facilitation_cases for the authenticated role even after grants. has_role/is_admin function privileges and table grants are now in place (verified via has_table_privilege/has_function_privilege), so the residual is most likely a planner-side issue with the SECURITY DEFINER subquery being invoked from the storage schema. Needs further root-cause: either (a) simplify the storage policy to call a single SECURITY DEFINER helper (e.g. has_facilitation_case_access(case_id)) that returns boolean, OR (b) check storage role search_path / type visibility for public.app_role.
remaining_failure.smallest_safe_fix: Add a SECURITY DEFINER helper public.facilitation_case_visible(_user uuid, _case uuid) returns boolean and rewrite the two fevd_* storage policies to call it. Confined to a single migration; does not change feature scope, does not introduce any send path.
